newsSinceJun6: No company-specific material news since June 6. Today's +5.99% move (YTD now ~40%) tracks the broad Japan AI/semiconductor-equipment rally that has driven the Nikkei to records (68,000 broken June 3; Advantest/Tokyo Electron-led push toward 70,000) - a flow/momentum move, not a fundamental catalyst. No earnings, contracts, capacity or analyst events found; the last notable company item (4,000th laser saw milestone) dates to February 2026.

sources.label: Al Jazeera - Japan's stock market hits new record as AI boom gathers steam (Jun 3, 2026)
FXEmpire - Nikkei 225 Forecast: Advantest and Tokyo Electron lead push toward 70,000
DISCO Corporation - 2026 corporate news (laser saw 4,000-unit milestone, Feb 2026)

verdicts.rankRationale: On a node-centrality lens Disco's structural position is unchanged - near-monopoly (~70%) in dicing/grinding feeding HBM thinning and chiplet packaging - so a flow-driven +6% sector pop with zero new fundamentals does not move its rank; small negative drift acknowledges YTD jumping from 32% to 40% without new evidence.
verdicts.bull: Every HBM stack and chiplet package passes through Disco-class grinders/dicers; if 2027 advanced-packaging capacity adds accelerate, Disco's tool intensity rises regardless of which OSAT or memory maker wins.
verdicts.neutral: Thesis intact but nothing new since June 6; today's move is the Japan AI-rally beta, so the node-centrality score should simply drift with the prior assessment.
verdicts.bear: Disco's AI exposure is more diluted (edge, power, general semis) than pure HBM/CoWoS names, and a 40% YTD rerate on no news means any tool-order digestion quarter hits the multiple hard.
verdicts.primaryCatalyst: Preliminary Q1 FY2026 shipment/sales disclosure in early July confirming HBM/advanced-packaging tool demand.
verdicts.primaryRisk: Momentum unwind in the crowded Japan AI-semicap trade plus a pause in memory-maker capex digesting the multiple.

verdicts.rankRationale: This is a crowding-sensitive alpha list that already flags Disco as 'well recognized / high expectations'; a +6% flow-driven day with no new CPO or packaging evidence and YTD swelling from 32% to 40% erodes residual alpha, partially reversing yesterday's up-move on price action alone.
verdicts.bull: Disco remains an unavoidable toll on photonics and CPO substrate thinning/dicing, and any concrete CPO production ramp news would justify re-rating it back up the list.
verdicts.neutral: Yesterday's up-move rested on bottleneck linkage, not new contracts; today's no-news pop simply pulls forward that recognition, leaving the alpha case roughly where it was but pricier.
verdicts.bear: At ~$48B market cap, 40% YTD and consensus-loved status, Disco is the opposite of a hidden bottleneck name - the asymmetric upside this list rewards has largely been arbitraged away by the Nikkei AI rally.
verdicts.primaryCatalyst: Company-specific evidence of CPO/photonics tool orders or a capacity announcement tied to 2027 advanced-packaging buildout.
verdicts.primaryRisk: Post-rerating chase: entering after a no-news 6% pop in a crowded Japan AI trade leaves poor risk/reward if sector flows reverse.
